purpose: settings.reverse_digraph=true set before loading: parse_graph reverses every edge after read_graphml (graphml_parser.py:18-19, reached via pyreason.py:577), so the fixture's A->B becomes B->A and the same rule 'derived(y) <-1 rel(x,y)' now derives derived(A) at t=1 and t=2, trace-node grounding [[B,A]] — the flip of both the derived node and the grounded edge against the twin reverse-digraph-default is the reversal evidence (verified at authoring — the harness judges each case within one knob position); knob readback True, get_time 3. The flag is also snapshotted into the Program as reverse_graph (pyreason.py:1609), but engine-side that copy is only stored and threaded (interpretation.py:69/:232/:242, mirrored in the fp and parallel kernels) and never consumed in any kernel body (grep-verified at the pin), so the forwarded-to-engine class has no behavioral observable beyond the snapshot — the nondefault-true-reversed and read-at-load-time classes.
provenance: spine case against docs/surface.md setting:reverse_digraph nondefault-true-reversed + read-at-load-time
